channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill at least 22 as Vatican envoy visits Christians for pre-Christmas Mass
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip overnight and into Sunday killed at least 22 people, including five children, Palestinian medical officials said, while Gaza's small Christian community celebrated a pre-Christmas Mass.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rome's iconic Trevi Fountain reopens after renovation work in time for the Jubilee Holy Year
item.description: Rome’s iconic Trevi Fountain reopened Sunday after three months of renovations on the 18th-century monument that’s one of the Italian capital’s main attractions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prominent Lebanese figure meets Syrian insurgent who led Assad's ouster, seeking better relations
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A prominent Lebanese politician held talks on Sunday with the insurgent who led the overthrow of Syria's President Bashar Assad, with both expressing hope for a new era in relations.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German Christmas market attack suspect in custody under murder charges
item.description: The man suspected of driving a car through crowds at a German Christmas market is in custody on murder charges, police said Sunday. On Friday, Taleb al-Abdulmohsen, a resident of Germany originally from Saudi Arabia, allegedly rammed a car into a busy holiday market in the German city of Magdeburg.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Stampedes in Nigeria leave at least 32 dead during Christmas food distribution
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — The death toll from stampedes during two Christmas charity events in Nigeria has increased from 13 to 32, police said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to track Santa's flight around the world this year
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in what appeared to be "friendly fire", but were found alive, the U.S. military said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: The U.S. military says two U.S. Navy pilots have been shot down over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope to skip outdoor Sunday prayer after catching cold days ahead of Christmas Eve, Day Masses
item.description: Pope Francis is suffering from a cold and will give the Sunday blessing ahead of a busy week that includes Christmas Eve and Day Masses and the start of the Holy Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's Mayotte struggles to recover as cyclone overwhelms hospitals
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — One week after its worst cyclone in nearly a century, and a day after a testy presidential visit, France’s impoverished Indian Ocean territory of Mayotte is still grappling with counting the dead, restoring essential services and aiding a beleaguered population.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Controversy plagued UN agency that employed Oct. 7 terrorists facing new problems as country redirects funding
item.description: UN agencies are reportedly offering themselves as replacements to scandal hit UNRWA that's been riddled with accusations of terrorism since the Hamas terror massacre in southern Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Russian official says a Ukrainian strike with US-supplied missiles kills 6
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian attack Friday on a town in Russia’s Kursk border region using U.S.-supplied missiles killed six people, including a child, a senior local official said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's Trudeau reshuffles his Cabinet as resignation calls mount and new election threat looms
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's chances of staying on in power have become more tenuous after the opposition party that backed his government for years announced it will vote no confidence in the government when Parliament resumes.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 2 dead, 60 hurt after car drives into German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities suspect was an attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctors Without Borders points to 'clear signs of ethnic cleansing' by Israel in Gaza
item.description: Doctors Without Borders said Israel is committing “clear signs of ethnic cleansing” in its war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Biden missing in action as Turkey inches closer to full-blown war against US-allied Kurds in Syria
item.description: Amid the end of the nearly 14 years of civil war in Syria, Turkish strongman Recep Tayyip Erdoğan is set to rekindle a new war in northern Syria against America’s main ally, the Syrian Kurds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. universities have issued guidance to their international students urging them to be back on campus before the Jan. 20 inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Support for quick end to Russia-Ukraine war grows in US: Gallup
item.description: Americans' desire to speed up the end of the Russia-Ukraine war has ticked up, a new Gallup survey found.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs if EU doesn't buy more oil, gas
item.description: President-elect Trump on Friday threatened tariffs in the European Union (EU) unless the bloc purchases large quantities of American oil and gas to make up for its growing deficit with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: GOP seeks ‘maximum pressure’ on Iran with nuclear clock ticking
item.description: Republican lawmakers want a return to "maximum pressure" on Iran in the second Trump administration and are widely open to the idea of strikes on Iranian nuclear sites if it’s the only way to prevent Tehran from wielding nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: US diplomats in Damascus for first time in more than 10 years following fall of Assad regime
item.description: U.S. officials are in Syria with goals to advance Aqaba principles and seek information on American citizens who disappeared under the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to travel to Italy for meetings with pope, Meloni in January
item.description: President Biden will travel to Italy in January for meetings with Pope Francis, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Italian President Sergio Mattarella. The trip, from Jan. 9 to 12, will likely be his last foreign trip as president before President-elect Trump is sworn in on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Pentagon says the number of US troops in Syria is much higher than previously reported
item.description: The Pentagon said Thursday that there are about 2,000 U.S. Troops in Syria, a number that is more than double the 900 troops reported previously by the DoD for months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon doubled the number of US troops in Syria 'months' ahead of Assad’s fall
item.description: The U.S. for months has had more than 2,000 troops inside Syria, more than double the 900 troops the Defense Department previously said are on the ground there, the Pentagon’s top spokesperson revealed Thursday. The increased force presence has “been going on for a while,” with press secretary Maj.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: 13 die as an Indian navy speedboat crashes into a passenger ferry off Mumbai
item.description: At least 13 people were killed after an Indian navy speedboat crashed into a ferry that was carrying more than 100 people; the ferry was on its way to a tourist destination off Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says Russia ready to compromise with Trump on Ukraine war
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he was ready to make a compromise regarding ending the war in Ukraine in possible talks with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frenchman found guilty in horrific rape trial that shocked the world
item.description: A Frenchman accused of drugging his wife of 50 years and inviting strangers over to rape her – while he filmed the assaults – was found guilty on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blast kills 2 Mexican soldiers and wounds 5 others in suspected booby trap by drug cartel
item.description: While approaching, they spotted three dismembered bodies near the encampment, which appeared to be abandoned. But as they drew closer, a buried device exploded and struck the soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation
